steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The student wants to use a quotation from Naguib specifically to explain what the sculpture represents. The correct choice must include a direct quote from Naguib about the sculpture's meaning (not her general artistic goals), and attribute it to her.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Naguib, whose works include a sculpture that is a large, curved strip of wood, has explained that she wants her work to create a dialogue with audiences.: No quotation is included, so this fails the task of using a quotation from Naguib. ✗
  • Choice B — Inspired by the sistrum played by Egyptian priestesses, Here I Have Returned symbolizes "women who have lifted and supported Egyptian society and culture," according to Naguib.: This includes Naguib's direct quotation about what the sculpture represents and correctly attributes it to her. ✓
  • Choice C — According to Naguib, the curved strip of wood used in Here I Have Returned was inspired by the sistrum, a musical instrument played by ancient Egyptian priestesses in ceremonies.: This paraphrases the inspiration for the sculpture's form but includes no quotation from Naguib, so it fails the task. ✗
  • Choice D — Naguib, the sculptor of Here I Have Returned, wants her works to "engage audiences in a dialogue about power, agency, and social transformation.": This uses a quotation from Naguib, but the quote describes her general artistic goals, not what this sculpture represents. ✗
B is correct.
